decisions.summary: Clean rename pi→ohmypi across union types, runtime registry, DB enum, routing, ToS gate, files, and frontend — NOT an in-place swap. Keep read-only back-compat: EXTEND the EXISTING normalizeHarness() at src/lib/overdeck/conversations.ts:851 (do not fork it) to map legacy 'pi'→'ohmypi' on read, and the on-disk session-path detector stays harness-agnostic so existing Pi transcripts still parse.

Execute the type rename as widen→migrate→narrow so every intermediate commit is green: (a) widen union to transient 4-value 'claude-code'|'pi'|'ohmypi'|'codex' and extend the existing normalizeHarness(), routing the inline duplicate harness validation at src/lib/database/conversations-db.ts:142 through it (single source of truth); (b) migrate each subsystem to 'ohmypi'; (c) narrow union back to 3 values, keeping 'pi' only in normalizeHarness() for legacy reads. The narrow bead's compiler errors are the residual-site finder. NOTE: conversations-db.ts DOES exist (the orchestrator's 'phantom file' note was wrong) — line 142 is a real inline validation that must be collapsed into normalizeHarness().

omp is a superset FORK of the same pi-mono: --mode rpc, the extension model, and JSONL session shape are preserved; but binary (pi→omp, no pi alias), npm package (@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent→@oh-my-pi/pi-coding-agent), config/auth dir (~/.pi→~/.omp/agent), and the extension import package all change. Exact flags/hook-names/paths are NOT in upstream prose — the gated foundation bead discovers them from `omp --help` + the installed package and records them in docs/ohmypi-contract.md. Downstream beads consume the contract; they never guess.
Verify parser parity against REAL omp output: bead 1 installs omp, runs a real --mode rpc session, and commits the captured transcript as the cost/conversation parser fixture. No assumed-v3 parity.

Parity + two BOUNDED low-risk wins land in this issue: (a) surface omp's additional routable providers; (b) capture extra omp usage/cost fields + tool-call detail in the parsers (PAN-1912 data half). All higher-risk omp features (LSP/DAP, hashline edits, subagents, browser/search, ACP, model-control UIs) are deferred to filed follow-up issues.

No-loss audit is a hard requirement, enumerate-before + verify-after: bead 2 writes docs/ohmypi-no-loss-audit.md enumerating all 20 Pi surface rows + 4 defects; bead 16 is a closing gate that verifies every row has a home (preserved/replaced/retired) and every defect is dispositioned.

Four in-flight Pi defects are explicitly dispositioned, not dropped: PAN-1859 (RED main resume→FIFO test) resolved in bead 4; PAN-1833 (extension cwd detection) resolved in bead 5; PAN-1827 (blank pi conversation view) resolved in bead 8; PAN-1912 split — tool-call data in bead 14, frontend Tools-toggle UI carried forward as a filed follow-up.

Auto-file follow-up GH issues (bead 19) from the capabilities doc's Proposed-Follow-ups table; the operator authorizes agents to file issues freely. Rename `pan pi-auth`→`pan ohmypi-auth`, keeping `pan pi-auth` as a hidden deprecated alias for one release.

hazards.summary: main is RED (PAN-1859: agent-spawning.test.ts resume→Pi FIFO test fails). The work agent builds on a red base, and a red main silently empties the merge gate.
hazards.mitigation: Bead 4 must land a GREEN ohmypi resume→FIFO delivery test (it is the rename of the very surface that is failing). Verify CI conclusion (not just HEAD sha) before relying on readyForMerge.

hazards.summary: omp session transcript format may differ from Pi v3 — cost parser and conversation parser break silently if assumed.
hazards.mitigation: Bead 1 captures a REAL omp --mode rpc transcript as a committed fixture; beads 7/8 are written and unit-tested against it. The contract doc records any v3 divergence.

hazards.summary: omp's exact CLI flags, extension import package, hook names, and OAuth/config path are not enumerated in upstream prose.
hazards.mitigation: Bead 1 discovers them from `omp --help`/subcommand help + the installed package's TS types and records each in docs/ohmypi-contract.md with a stated fallback for any missing pi-equivalent flag. Downstream beads cite the contract, never guess.

hazards.summary: ToS policy gate (canUseHarnessSync, harness-policy.ts:69) blocks harness+Anthropic+subscription. Renaming the key is easy to get subtly wrong (drop the block, or reorder around it).
hazards.mitigation: Re-key the SAME blocked cell to 'ohmypi' with posture unchanged; keep harness-policy.test.ts asserting the block. Never weaken or reorder (concerns.md).

hazards.summary: Transient widened 4-value union could leak past the narrow bead, leaving a dishonest 'pi' as a writable value.
hazards.mitigation: Bead 15 narrows the writable union to 3 values; 'pi' survives only inside normalizeHarness(); the no-loss-audit gate (bead 16) checks no writable 'pi' producer remains.

hazards.summary: Resume across a model/harness change corrupts context (concerns.md): old Pi sessions resumed under omp.
hazards.mitigation: Treat a pi→omp identity change like any harness change — fresh session + continue.json re-onboarding, never --resume across the boundary. Legacy Pi transcripts are read-only history, not resume targets.

hazards.summary: Skills/CLI lint (scripts/lint-skills.sh) cross-checks wrapper skills against `pan <verb> --help`; renaming pi-auth or adding ohmypi-auth can fail CI if a wrapper skill drifts.
hazards.mitigation: Bead 9 updates any skill referencing pi-auth in the same commit and keeps the deprecated alias documented; run `npm run lint` before done.

hazards.summary: Dashboard server code must not use execSync (freezes the event loop); doctor's checkCommand is execSync-based and CLI-only.
hazards.mitigation: Keep checkOhmypi CLI-only; any server-reachable ohmypi status check uses async exec/spawn (concerns.md).

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1989: Replace Pi harness with ohmypi and evaluate advanced features

Discovery complete. Confirmed omp = @oh-my-pi/pi-coding-agent (binary omp), a superset fork of pi-mono. Operator decisions: clean rename, install+capture real omp, doc+auto-file follow-ups, parity+bounded low-risk wins. Folded in orchestrator context: hard no-loss audit FR + disposition of PAN-1859/1833/1827/1912. 19-bead widen→migrate→narrow plan authored.

ohmypi conversation session resolution was changed back to pi

## Required action

Fix every blocking review finding, commit the fixes, then re-request review with:

`pan review request PAN-1989 -m "Fixed review issues"`

## REQUIRED: Fix the failing check, push, and request a new review

1. Read the error output above carefully
2. Fix the code causing the failure
3. Run the failing check locally to verify it passes
4. Commit every change
5. Invoke the /rebase-and-submit skill for PAN-1989 — this is an atomic task. Because verification already ran once (a PR exists), the skill will push your branch and run `pan review request PAN-1989 -m "Fixed test"` for you. NEVER curl `/api/review/...` or any dashboard endpoint — `pan review request` is the only supported re-entry point.

Do NOT stop between steps. Do NOT stop after pushing. Do NOT stop until `pan review request` has completed successfully.
